{{Quote|She was tall—at least two meters—and slender. Selonians are all very lithe and she was covered with relatively short black fur that glistened a silver-blue when the light hit it right. Definitely gorgeous, definitely humanoid, but definitely not human.|Corran Horn to Mirax Terrik and Gavin Darklighter|<ref name="XWWG" />
}}
'''Chertyl Ruluwoor''' was a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Selonian/Legends|Selonian]] sent to [[Corran Horn/Legends|Corran Horn's]] [[Corellian Security Force|CorSec]] unit for training under a cultural exchange program. In the traditional leadup to the annual CorSec Officers' Ball the younger Horn got to know her, eventually taking her to the event. 

==Biography==
One tradition of the Officer's Ball was a raffle that the males would buy into to "win" a date with a volunteer female officer, with the proceeds being donated to the CorSec Survivors and Orphans Fund. That year, Chertyl offered herself as the prize and Corran rigged the pool to guarantee that he would be her escort. At that time, he was the only single man on the force and, feeling depressed over [[Hal Horn|his father's]] recent death, decided that other officers would not be separated from their loved ones for the evening.<ref name="XWWG" />

He went all out to make her evening special, renting a [[Limousine/Legends|repulsorlimo]] and using an [[X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-Wing]] to fly across the planet to buy her favorite flowers from the only florist who had them available. The two hit it off and became involved in a brief but passionate relationship which was complicated by very different long term relationship goals and incompatible body chemistry. After an intimate evening they both developed uncomfortable rashes, and subsequently discovered that Corran's sweat was mildly acidic, which irritated her skin, while he was allergic to her fur.<ref name="XWWG" />

Corran would later acknowledge that, though they were personally very compatible, they both knew that they were living a fantasy. They parted ways amicably and remained great friends. He would remember his involvement with Chertyl fondly, describing the relationship as being like planning to throw out some rocks and finding among them were [[Corusca gem/Legends|Corusca gems]] that would fit together to form a beautiful sculpture.<ref name="XWWG" />

During their time flying with [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]], [[Gavin Darklighter]] asked Corran about relationships with non-humans, prompted by his interest in the [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] [[Asyr Sei'lar]]. In response, Corran's former CorSec partner, [[Iella Wessiri Antilles|Iella Wessiri]], would relate the story about his difficulties with Chertyl to Gavin and [[Mirax Terrik Horn|Mirax Terrik]]. Despite his embarrassment over the story, Corran encouraged Gavin to give his relationship a try and see where things went.<ref name="XWWG" />

==Appearance==
Ruluwoor was described as being over two meters tall and lithe. She had short black fur, which gleamed a silver-blue in the correct light and was considered very attractive. While attending the CorSec Officers' Ball, she wore a slinky gown and a necklace of [[Silver/Legends|silver]] and aquamarine, as well as a bracelet that matched the necklace.<ref name="XWWG" />

==Behind the scenes==
Chertyl Ruluwoor was first mentioned in the novel ''[[X-Wing: Wedge's Gamble]]'', which was written by [[Michael A. Stackpole]] and published in [[1996]].<ref name="XWWG" />
